top.since: Founded in 2018 · Headquartered in São Paulo, Brazil

home.headline: A shared agenda for the energy that moves Latin America.
home.lead: ALAGER brings together associations, generators and institutions from Argentina to Mexico to coordinate public policy, defend investment in renewables and represent the sector where the decisions are actually made.
home.mapBody: Thirteen countries, more than 240 members and a combined operating capacity of 38 GW. Click a country to see the local representation.

pillars.b: Legislative monitoring, technical contributions to regulatory consultations, and permanent dialogue with energy-sector authorities.
Alignment among national associations for joint positions in international fora and energy-integration treaties.
Technical studies, regulatory briefings and market data distributed exclusively to members.
Institutional representation before governments, multilaterals and financiers to preserve regulatory predictability.

home.voiceQuote: “Latin America's energy transition will not be solved country by country. ALAGER exists so the sector can speak with one voice when it has to.”
home.joinTitle: Join the network that's shaping the region's energy future.
home.joinBody: Members get access to market intelligence, technical working groups, and ALAGER's direct representation in regional and multilateral fora.

about.headline: A non-profit association in service of Latin America's energy transition.
about.lead: Founded in 2018 by national renewable-generation associations, ALAGER operates as a regional representation body. It receives no public funds, is sustained by its members, and follows governance practices audited annually.
about.mission: Integrate, coordinate and defend the interests of Latin American institutions and companies committed to the development of renewable energy.
about.vision: A Latin America where renewables form the backbone of the energy mix, regulated with predictability and financed with long-term instruments.

values.b: Positions built on technical grounds — no party-political or individual commercial alignment.
Representation of every renewable source — solar, wind, biomass, hydro, geothermal, hydrogen.
Financial statements, minutes and public positions available to members.
Institutional dialogue that outlasts political cycles and keeps the sector aligned.

about.timelineTitle: Eight years coordinating the region's renewable sector.
about.governanceBody: ALAGER has an annual General Assembly, a Deliberative Council with parity across countries, and an Executive Board elected for two-year terms. Official positions are decided by consensus, with dissenting votes published.

energy.headline: Five sources, one transition.
energy.lead: Each renewable source plays a specific role in the Latin American mix — generation profiles, capital costs, regulatory demands and bottlenecks of its own. We follow all of them through dedicated technical workstreams.
energy.calcTitle: Estimate the avoided impact of a renewable project
energy.calcLead: Use the simulator below to estimate avoided emissions, equivalent households served and trees planted. Simplified, indicative calculation only.

blog.headline: Regulatory intelligence and institutional reading of the sector.
blog.lead: Regulatory analysis, official positions, sector briefings and event coverage. Open content — and, for members, in-depth technical reports.

join.headline: Being an ALAGER member means taking part in the decisions that shape Latin America's energy transition.
join.lead: Three membership tiers, all with a voice in the technical working groups. The tier defines the intelligence package, the number of seats in fora and the level of institutional representation.
join.formLead: Fill in the form below and our team will be in touch within five business days with the documentation needed for your tier.
join.fMessage: How do you intend to contribute to ALAGER?
join.fOk: We received your submission. The executive secretariat will reply within 5 business days.

footer.tagline: Coordinating Latin America's energy transition since 2018.
footer.addr: Av. Paulista, 1st floor · São Paulo · Brazil
footer.newsletterDesc: Monthly analysis, position papers and regulatory calendar. No noise.
